steps.title: Spot the genetic evidence
steps.content: The two condors hatched from eggs laid by females in the enclosure, yet they weren't genetically similar enough to any males in the enclosure to be those males' offspring. This means the evidence pointed away from any male in the enclosure being their biological father.

steps.title: Infer the conclusion
steps.content:
If the offspring came from females but probably weren't fathered by any male in the enclosure, the scientists could conclude that those mothers probably reproduced asexually—through parthenogenesis—to produce offspring with all-maternal DNA.

The other answers either shift focus to the mothers' origins (A) or introduce unsupported speculation about wild behavior and conditional triggers (B, D).

C is correct.
